title: Crondre

'''Crondre''' was a small, [[Water/Legends|icy]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] located in the [[Dufilvian sector/Legends|Dufilvian sector]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. Its surface consisted of icy glaciers.

The [[Planet/Legends|planet]] was colonized generations before the start of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Its residents believed strongly in a peaceful existence until it was forcibly conquered by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] shortly before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|destruction]] of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]]. The planet was heavily damaged by the ongoing war.

The [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] used the planet as a medical supply depot, designed to flash-freeze perishable medical supplies. The storage facility was located in a hollowed out glacier, similar to [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. A series of [[Fabritech/Legends|Fabritech]] [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generators]] protected the facility from attack, as well as a [[V-150 Planet Defender/Legends|v-150 Planet Defender]] [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]]. A [[Golan II Space Defense SpaceGun|Golan II]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]] protected the world from [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]].<ref name="TTS">''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]''</ref>

Crondre was the site of a diversionary [[Battle of Crondre|attack]] by the forces of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], whose real target was [[Ukio/Legends|Ukio]], during his campaign against the New Republic.

==Behind the scenes==
The PC game [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] mistakenly places Crondre in the [[Jospro sector/Legends|Jospro sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Additionally, ''Rebellion's'' in-game encyclopedia entry for Crondre describes the planet as having once been beautiful before being ravaged from its involvement in the Galactic Civil War. This seems contradictory to its description as an icy world in the novel ''[[The Last Command]]''.
